author: jtraglia
content: So this is already sort of the case btw. Builder exits use a target=2 and max=16 per payload. The only way a builder can be in the withdrawal sweep is if its exited.

author: barnabasbusa
content:
so basically: 
```python
def apply_deposit_for_builder(
    state: BeaconState,
    pubkey: BLSPubkey,
    withdrawal_credentials: Bytes32,
    amount: uint64,
    signature: BLSSignature,
    slot: Slot,
) -> None:
    builder_pubkeys = [b.pubkey for b in state.builders]
    if pubkey not in builder_pubkeys:
        # Verify the deposit signature (proof of possession) which is not checked by the deposit contract
        if is_valid_deposit_signature(pubkey, withdrawal_credentials, amount, signature):
            add_builder_to_registry(state, pubkey, withdrawal_credentials, amount, slot)
    else:
        # Increase balance by deposit amount
        builder_index = builder_pubkeys.index(pubkey)
        state.builders[builder_index].balance += amount
        # Re-arm the withdrawability delay so a top-up to an exited builder is not
        # immediately sweepable (prevents crowding the validator sweep).
        if state.builders[builder_index].withdrawable_epoch != FAR_FUTURE_EPOCH:
            state.builders[builder_index].withdrawable_epoch = (
                get_current_epoch(state) + MIN_BUILDER_WITHDRAWABILITY_DELAY
            )
```
```

author: jtraglia
content: Ah I should have opened the doc first. The attack is repeated top-ups to an exited builder. This is a good find. We knew that validator withdrawals could be limited to one btw (see https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/4832) but I agree this would be pretty easy to abuse. <@586161934425128960>'s suggestion of limiting deposits to 8 is a good solution.

author: nflaig
content: is that relevant here, don't you only need 1 exited builder to do this?

author: barnabasbusa
content: yeah Brechy's suggestion to reset the clock is probably the easiest and best solution here

author: jtraglia
content: Yup this is a good solution as well. An attacker could only sustain this for so long.

author: jtraglia
content: <@586161934425128960> do you feel that this is better than adjusting limits? What would you think about doing both?

author: jtraglia
content: Regardless, I think resetting the builder's withdrawal epoch on top-ups is a good idea.

author: nflaig
content: I don't really understand why potuz wants such a high max, I don't think we need 256, having 8/64 should be fine? but doesn't matter if we reset the withdrawal epoch

author: jtraglia
content: To expand a bit... it would cost ~$150k/minute to sustain this. If we reset the withdrawal epoch on top-ups, they wouldn't be able to re-use the same ETH to do this, so an attacker would be limited by the total amount of ETH they have. I would say this is sufficient & we don't need to lower the builder deposit target/limit in 8282.
